---
title: "LRP60: SES-adjusted ITT analysis of word reading (W)"

date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

::: {.callout-warning title="Warning: Preliminary model output"}

This model output is preliminary and likely to change as the model evolves and further data are received.

If the model was not fitted in "reporting" mode, the results will be more approximate than where additional sampling has been performed.

:::

```{python}
# | echo: false
import json
from pathlib import Path

import arviz as az
import pandas as pd

_here = Path(".")
with open(_here / "config.json") as f:
    config = json.load(f)
trace = az.from_netcdf(_here / "trace.nc")
tau_summary = pd.read_csv(_here / "tau_summary.csv")
```

## Overview

LRP60 is a robustness check on the primary word-reading ITT model (LRP52). It
uses the same randomised phase (time 1 $\rightarrow$ time 2) and the same
word-reading outcome (`ewrswr`), but adds three standardised baseline
socio-economic / home-literacy covariates:

- mother's years of post-16 education (`mumedupost16`);
- father's years of post-16 education (`dadedupost16`);
- age when the child was first read to (`agebooks`).

The estimand remains the total effect of randomised group assignment $G$ on
$W_{\text{post}}$. The adjustment tests whether the word-reading effect is
materially sensitive to small randomisation imbalances in these baseline
characteristics.

## Model

The linear predictor is

$$
\eta_i = \alpha
       + \tau\, G_i
       + \gamma_W\, \text{logit}_*(W_{\mathrm{pre},i}, N_W)
       + \sum_{k \in \{R,E,L,P,B,F,T\}} \gamma_k\, \text{logit}_*(k_{\mathrm{pre},i}, N_k)
       + \beta_{\mathrm{mum}}\, \text{mumedu}_{\mathrm{std},i}
       + \beta_{\mathrm{dad}}\, \text{dadedu}_{\mathrm{std},i}
       + \beta_{\mathrm{books}}\, \text{agebooks}_{\mathrm{std},i}.
$$

The likelihood is the same Beta-Binomial word-reading likelihood as LRP52:
$W_{\text{post},i} \sim \text{BetaBinomial}(N_W, \mu_i \kappa, (1-\mu_i)\kappa)$,
with $\mu_i = \sigma(\eta_i)$. The adjustment coefficients use the shared
`gamma_cross` prior, $\mathcal{N}(0, 0.3)$, on standardised covariates.

::: {.callout-note title="Interpretation"}

Because assignment was randomised, this adjustment is a robustness and precision
check, not the main identification strategy. If $\tau$ is stable relative to
LRP52, the headline word-reading effect is not materially explained by these
SES / home-literacy variables. If $\tau$ shifts, the project-review summary
should treat the original unadjusted estimate more cautiously.

:::

![Model diagram](model_graph.svg){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

## Shared priors

![alpha](prior_alpha.svg){width=40%}
![tau](prior_tau.svg){width=40%}
![gamma_own](prior_gamma_own.svg){width=40%}
![gamma_cross](prior_gamma_cross.svg){width=40%}
![kappa](prior_kappa.svg){width=40%}

## Sampling diagnostics

```{python}
# | echo: false
diag = pd.read_csv(_here / "diagnostics.csv", index_col=0)
diag
```

![Trace](trace_plot.png){.lightbox}

![Energy](energy_plot.png){.lightbox}

![Pair plot](pair_plot.png){.lightbox}

![Posterior](posterior_plot.png){.lightbox}

## Posterior predictive check

![Posterior predictive](posterior_predictive_check.png){.lightbox}

## Treatment-effect summary

```{python}
# | echo: false
tau_summary
```

- `tau_logit_*`: posterior mean and $(1-\alpha)/2$, $1-(1-\alpha)/2$ quantiles of $\tau$ on the logit scale.
- `tau_prob_*`: implied marginal effect on the probability scale, evaluated at the sample-mean baseline ($\alpha$, $\gamma_{\text{own}}$ held at their posterior means). This is a point-estimate *sensitivity* summary, not a causal contrast.
- `prob_tau_pos`: $P(\tau > 0)$.

## LOO-PSIS

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: asis
print("```\n" + open(_here / "loo.txt").read() + "\n```")
```

## Configuration

```{python}
# | echo: false
config
```
